div1.entryFree: Swerve, to leave the due or intended course, to go astray, to be inconstant: the cause of this fair gift in me is wanting, and so my patent back again is --ing, Sonn. 87, 8. the fairest youth that ever made eye s. Wint. IV, 4, 385. constant in spirit, not --ing with the blood, H5 II, 2, 133. I have offended reputation, a most unnoble --ing, Ant. III, 11, 50. but, alas, I s. Cymb. V, 4, 129. With from, == to deviate from: that you s. not from the smallest article of it, Meas. IV, 2, 107. if I be false, or s. a hair from truth, Troil. III, 2, 191.
